channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Warhammer 40K Characters Review - Berehk Stornbrow - Twin Lance - Tyranid Prime
item.content:encoded:
Over the last 2 months, we've seen 2 campaign sets and new miniatures for the Ultramarines, Red Corsairs, and Aeldari Corsairs, which are building up to the launch of the 11th edition of Warhammer 40,000. Bridging the gap between The Maelstrom and the next campaign set, 3 xenos races are getting brand new characters to help with the battles that are surely coming. In this article, we check out the new characters for the Leagues of Votann, Tau Empire, and Tyranids.

We've been covering Warhammer 40K for a while on the site, and you can check out our full Warhammer 40K Guide, our preview for the Leviathan box that launched the latest edition of the rules, or the most recent wave of Ultramarines and Space Marine reinforcements.

Warhammer 40K characters are usually heroic heroes of legend, ploughing through unnamed enemies on the battlefield. Some of these characters can join units, adding special rules and abilities to those they join. Others offer offensive capabilities or supporting abilities. The most powerful characters are named characters, with history and background in the setting, like Captain Titus from Space Marine II, or Huron Blackheart, who's been firmly established in 40K lore for a while.

Berehk Stornbrow

Berehk Stormbrow is a Leagues of Votann leader who can only join units of Cthonian Beserks. Even though Berehk strides around half-naked, he's pretty durable with 5 wounds, a 5+ invulnerable save, and then a subsequent 4+ feels no pain save. Which is helpful as Berehk only has melee attacks, so you'll need to protect him as he closes the distance to make use of his Kromlok’s Revenge mining axe. Kromlok's Revenge has 2 attack profiles, either a 10 attacks strength 4 with -2 AP and 4 damage, or 7 attacks, strength 8 with -2 AP and 2 damage. He also gets 2 extra attacks from his warpforge gauntlets, with strength 6, -2 AP, and 1 damage.

Berehk also gives the melee weapons of his and the unit he's in Sustained Hits 1, which deals 1 extra damage for each 6 rolled in attack, which combines well with the Berserk's concussion mauls and Berehk's Kromlok's Revenge as they also have Anti-Vehicle and Anti-Monster special rules, which make scoring critical hits against those targets easier. He and the unit he's in also get a free Heroic Intervention stratagem to use, which lets Berehk charge a unit that finishes a charge within 6 inches of them.

His model depicts him presenting a huge Ork head with spine still attached, clearly showing the types of targets that Berehk likes to lead his unit against.

Twin Lance 

Ri’Lantar and Ri’Locai, known as the Twin Lance, are the Tau Empire's new exemplars of aggressive combat. They come equipped with a range of new gear to bring the fight to their enemies. Wearing new Warmaker Battlesuits, which makes them rapid with a 10-inch move, an 8-inch scout ability to start them up the table, and a retro thrusters ability which allows them to make a 6-inch move or fall back at the end of the fight phase. The new suits also make them pretty durable with a normal 2+ save and a 4+ invulnerable save, along with toughness 6 and 8 wounds each.

Weapons-wise, the Twin Lance are packing, with both armed with pulse pistols, shardstorm burst systems (which have D6 strength 5 attacks), and twin pulse blaster-armed gun drones. But their main weapons are the Fusion Eliminator that Ri’Lantar carries, which has 2 strength 10 attacks, -4 AP with D6 damage, and the Ion Scattercannon that Ri’Locai wields, which has 2 modes of fire, maxing at 4 strength 8 attacks, -3 AP, 3 damage if using the hazardous mode. 

Both of the Twin Lance's main armaments have abilities that add value at closer range, which is 9 inches for both the Fusion Eliminator (with Melta 2 that adds extra damage) and the Ion Scattercannon (with Rapid Fire 2 that adds more attacks), and that suits them both perfectly, because they want to get in close. Both Twin Lances' ranged weapons can also be used in close combat, with extra attack melee profiles on top of ther pulse pistols. They also gain sustained hits for extra damage and ignore cover if they target the closest enemy with their ranged attacks. Their profiles close out with Neocapacitor Shields, which lets them select a non-vehicle/non-monster enemy at the start of the charge phase within 12 inches, and forces them to make a battle-shock test, and subtrack 1 from their charge rolls.

The Twin Lance miniatures look incredible, sporting cool poses that suit the fact that they're going to always be in the thick of the action on the tabletop. Their gun drones also have ribbons that match those the Twin Lance wear, giving their drones a unique look against others in your force.

Tyranid Prime

The Tyranids have also adapted to the changing face of war, tearing the wings off the original Tyranid Prime and giving it a range of melee weapons. This new Prime can join units of Hormagaunts, Termagants, or Tyranid Warriors and have the movement to match the speedy Hormagaunts. The Prime comes with an Aggressive Leader-Beast ability that allows the unit they're in to surge D6 inches towards the nearest enemy, if any models in the unit were destroyed as the result of a shooting attack, including to within engagement range of an enemy, but not if they are already within engagement range. So your opponent will need to be careful when attacking the Prime's unit, otherwise they could find themselves face-to-face with it shortly after.

Once in combat, the Prime has 3 different weapon choices that increase in number of attacks, but decrease in strength, including the 4 attack rending claw with strength 8 damage 3, the 6 attack scything talons, with strength 6 damage 2, and the 8 attack lash whip, with strength 4 damage 1. This gives you optionality depending on what enemy you've targeted, and the Prime also gives all members of its unit Sustained Hits 1, which generates extra hits on a crit. So throwing the Prime in a unit with lots of attacks would be a good bet to generate more attacks, after pushing them into combat with Aggressive Leader-Beast.

The miniature for the new Tyranid Prime looks particularly aggressive, dynamically twisting its body, ready to strike with its lash whip.

Warhammer 40K Characters Final Thoughts?

Berehk and the Tyranid Prime are fine, but the Twin Lance really steals the limelight against the other 2 characters, with not only incredible miniatures, but also some insane mobility and ranged weapons in melee rules, along with showcasing the new Warmaker Battlesuits, which look awesome. The Twin Lance also progresses the Tau Empire narrative, with the pair taking the mantle from the now renegade Commander Farsight, which is always great to see.

The copies of the Warhammer 40K Characters, Berehk Stornbrow, Twin Lance, and Tyranid Prime used to produce this review were provided by Games Workshop.

Adam is a Tabletop Specialist for TechRaptor. He started writing for TechRaptor in 2017 and took over as Tabletop Editor in 2019 and has since stood down… More about Adam



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/warhammer-40k-characters-review-berehk-stornbrow-twin-lance-tyranid-prime
